rows.Definition: This is the Safety alert symbol. It is used to alert you to potential personal injury hazards. Obey all Safety messages that follow this symbol to avoid possible injury or death.
Indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, will result in death or serious injury.
Indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, could result in death or serious injury.
Indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, could result in minor or moderate injury.
Addresses practices not related to personal injury.

rows.Description: Electric Shock Hazard. Do not touch energized parts.
Inhalation Hazard. Keep head out of fumes and use proper ventilation.
Read manual before setup and/or use.
Fire Hazard. Keep flammable materials away during welding. Spatter can cause accidental fires.
Arc Ray Hazard. Wear welding helmet with properly rated filter lens.
Pacemaker Hazard. Welding processes may interfere with pacemakers. Consult doctor before use.

name: Warning Screen Problem and Remedy Table

rows.Remedy: The machine has thermally tripped. Allow the machine to cool.
The voltage input is below the specified limit. Inspect input voltage and ensure it is within machine limits.
The voltage input is higher than the specified limit. Inspect input voltage and ensure it is within machine limits.

rows.Problem: Wire Feed Motor Runs but Wire Does Not Feed Properly
rows.Possible Causes: 1. Insufficient wire feed pressure. 2. Incorrect wire feed roller size. 3. Damaged MIG Gun, cable, or liner assembly. 4. Feed Tensioner is too tight.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Increase wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17. 2. Flip roll to correct size. Follow the Feed Roller instructions on page 12. 3. Have a qualified technician inspect these parts and replace as necessary. 4. Loosen Feed Tensioner so it applies only enough pressure to prevent continued spinning after the Gun Trigger is released.

rows.Problem: Wire Creates a Bird's Nest During Operation
rows.Possible Causes: 1. Excess wire feed pressure. 2. Incorrect Contact Tip size. 3. MIG Gun Cable Connector not fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism. 4. Damaged liner.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Adjust wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17. 2. Replace with the proper tip for wire used. 3. Insert Gun Cable Connector properly. Follow steps 13 and 14 on page 13. 4. Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.

rows.Possible Causes: 1. Gun cable is severely bent and Wire Feed mechanism cannot feed wire. 2. Gun liner is clogged or worn. 3. Gun liner is too small for welding wire being used. 4. Wire is tangled on the spool. 5. Wire is not making contact with Feed Rollers. 6. Feed Roller is not making enough contact with wire or is crushing flux-cored wire.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Straighten Gun cable. 2. Check gun liner for obstruction. Replace if necessary. 3. Check that gun liner is correct size for wire. 4. Check wire for cross winding or tangled spool. 5. Check Feed Rollers and ensure correct groove for wire diameter is being used. 6. Check Feed Tensioner and ensure it is set properly.

rows.Possible Causes: 1. Wire not feeding properly. 2. Incorrect Contact Tip or liner size or excessive wear. 3. Incorrect wire feed speed. 4. Loose MIG Gun cable or ground cable. 5. Damaged MIG Gun or loose connection within Gun. 6. Incorrect polarity for process being run. 7. Gas coverage may be insufficient or too high. 8. Poor connection with workpiece.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. See first Troubleshooting section above. 2. Replace with the proper tip or liner size for wire used. 3. Adjust wire feed speed to achieve a more stable arc. 4. Check to ensure that all connections are tight. 5. Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary. 6. Ensure polarity is correct for operation: DCEP for MIG welding and DCEN for Flux-Cored self-shielded welding. 7. Ensure gas flow rate is set according to Settings Chart. Make sure MIG Gun Cable Connector is fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism with no O-Rings exposed. 8. Check the ground clamp connection to the workpiece and machine. Ensure the MIG Gun is properly secured.

rows.Possible Causes: 1. Incorrect line voltage. 2. Improper gauge or length of cord. 3. Not enough current.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation. 2. Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord. 3. Switch current to proper setting for metal thickness.

rows.Problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 1. Tripped thermal protection device.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods to help reduce wear on the welder. Refer to Duty Cycle (Duration of Use) on page 29.

rows.Problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 2. Faulty or improperly connected Trigger.
rows.Likely Solutions: 2. Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.

rows.Problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 3. Ground Clamp not attached to workpiece.
rows.Likely Solutions: 3. Attach Ground Clamp to workpiece.

rows.Problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 4. Shielding Gas not connected.
rows.Likely Solutions: 4. Connect shielding gas to Welder.

rows.Problem: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 1. Unit is not connected to outlet properly.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Verify the voltage at the outlet and the connection to the outlet.

rows.Problem: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 2. Outlet is unpowered.
rows.Likely Solutions: 2. Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if any are tripped, determine and remedy cause before resetting. Verify that the circuit is designed to supply the required input amperage as detailed in Specifications on page 7.

rows.Possible Causes: 1. Incorrect line voltage.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation.

rows.Possible Causes: 2. Improper gauge or length of cord.
rows.Likely Solutions: 2. Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord.

rows.Possible Causes: 1. Loose electrode cable or ground cable.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Check to ensure that all connections are tight.

rows.Possible Causes: 2. Damaged electrode holder or loose connection within electrode holder.
rows.Likely Solutions: 2. Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.

rows.Possible Causes: 3. Adjust current setting.
rows.Likely Solutions: 3. Make sure setting matches recommended setting on chart.

rows.Possible Causes: 4. Shielding gas getting low.
rows.Likely Solutions: 4. Replace shielding gas cylinder.

rows.120VAC / 60Hz: Mild Steel, Stainless Steel, Aluminum (with optional Spool Gun)
rows.240VAC / 60Hz: Mild Steel, Stainless Steel, Aluminum (with optional Spool Gun)

rows.120VAC / 60Hz: Mild Steel, Stainless Steel, Chrome Moly
rows.240VAC / 60Hz: Mild Steel, Stainless Steel, Chrome Moly

rows.Problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 1. Tripped thermal protection device. 2. Circuit supplies insufficient input voltage or amperage. 3. Faulty or improperly connected Trigger. 4. Machine is in low- or over-voltage protection. 5. Machine is in the incorrect mode.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. If a warning screen appears in the LCD Display window, Welder may have overheated and shut down. Stop and wait with the Power Switch ON for the Welder to cool. Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods. Refer to Duty Cycle on page 19. 2. Verify circuit supplies required input voltage and amperage per Specifications table; check LCD for warning screen. 3. Ensure gun connection is properly seated; qualified technician must check/replace Trigger. 4. Check input voltage is within specified range; if correct press Reset Button on back of machine. 5. Ensure correct process has been selected.

rows.Problem: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On
rows.Possible Causes: 1. Unit not connected to outlet properly. 2. Outlet is unpowered. 3. Plug does not have correct rating. 4. Circuit breaker tripped due to high input amperage. 5. Input Power Cord not seated properly.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Verify voltage at outlet and connection to outlet. 2. Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if tripped determine cause before resetting. 3. Make sure installed plug is correct rating; See Specifications on page 7. 4. Press Reset Button on back of machine to reset circuit breaker. 5. Ensure twist lock input Power Cord is fully secured.

rows.Problem: Wire Feeds, but Arc Does Not Ignite
rows.Possible Causes: 1. Improper ground connection. 2. Improperly sized Contact Tip. 3. Excessively worn Contact Tip. 4. Dirty Contact Tip.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Make certain workpiece is contacted properly by Ground Clamp and that workpiece is cleaned near ground clamp and welding location. 2. Verify Contact Tip is proper size for welding wire; replace if needed. 3. Check tip hole is not deformed or enlarged; replace if needed. 4. Properly clean Contact Tip.

rows.Possible Causes: 1. Shielding gas bottle empty. 2. Not enough or too much shielding gas. 3. Dirty workpiece. 4. Gun used too far away from workpiece. 5. Polarity incorrect for application. 6. Dirty welding wire introducing contamination.
rows.Likely Solutions: 1. Check gas bottle and replenish as necessary. 2. Check gas regulator to ensure proper flow. 3. Clean workpiece down to bare metal. 4. Check CTWD (contact tip to work distance) for proper procedure. 5. Check polarity and ensure DCEP for MIG and DCEN for Flux-Cored. 6. Ensure welding wire is clean and free of rust and residues.
